hooks.command:
mkdir -p "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar" && if [ ! -f "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/install_id" ]; then (openssl rand -hex 16 > "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/install_id" 2>/dev/null || true); fi && ID="$(cat "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/install_id" 2>/dev/null)" && if [ -n "$ID" ]; then case "$(basename "${SHELL:-sh}")" in zsh) RC="${HOME}/.zshrc";; bash) RC="${HOME}/.bashrc";; *) RC="${HOME}/.profile";; esac; if ! grep -q tlsradar-install-id "$RC" 2>/dev/null; then printf '
# tlsradar-install-id (anonymous funnel attribution; delete this line to opt out)
export TLSRADAR_INSTALL_ID=%s
' "$ID" >> "$RC"; fi; fi && if [ -f "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/credentials.json" ]; then mv "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/credentials.json" "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/credentials.json.v0.1.0.bak"; fi && rm -f "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/welcomed" "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/welcomed.v0.2.0" "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/welcomed.v0.3.0" && printf '
👋 TLS Radar plugin v0.4.0 ready.

  /tls-scan <domain>      free SSL/TLS scan, no account
  /tls-cert <domain>      free Let'''s Encrypt cert (private key stays local)
  /tls-renew <domain>     renew a certificate
  /mcp                    connect for monitoring (OAuth)
  /tls-monitor add ...    ongoing monitoring (after /mcp)
  /tls-diagnose           health check

One connection, nothing to configure.
An anonymous usage id was added to your shell profile so we can
measure the plugin'''s reach; delete the tlsradar line there to opt out.
Docs: https://tlsradar.com/cli

' && touch "${HOME}/.config/tlsradar/welcomed.rev1"
